event: transferred to the intensive care unit
ophthalmological examination bilateral loss of vision at the level of insecured light perception
normal pupillary responses to a light stimulus
neurological examination mild right-sided facial nerve paresis
multislice computed tomography scan without contrast showed hypodensity of the posterior white matter
MRI T2- and FLAIR-weighted images hyperintense signals in the white matter predominantly in the parietal and occipital regions and at the junctions of vascular watershed zones
methyldopa 500 mg three times a day
significant bilateral improvement of the visual function up to 1.0 of best-corrected visual acuity
visual field bilaterally expressed peripheral relative scotoma
visual field depressed sensitivity of the paracentral left visual field
discrete residual changes over the posterior horns of the side ventricles
continue with physical therapy for the paresis of the facial nerve
